name: Transit
description_short: Transit Federal Credit Union is a Valley Stream, NY-based credit union offering checking, savings, auto loans, and online banking services to local members.
description_long:
Transit Federal Credit Union operates as a federally chartered credit union serving the Valley Stream, New York community and surrounding service areas. Founded to enhance the financial well-being of its members, Transit FCU provides a traditional credit union model with membership-based access to financial products. The credit union is regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration), which publishes transaction and membership data for the institution. Transit FCU maintains a physical branch presence in Valley Stream and offers phone-based customer service at (718) 425-1312.

Transit FCU's service offerings include checking accounts (Share Draft Accounts), savings accounts (Regular Share Savings), money market accounts, and auto lending. The credit union advertises competitive auto loan rates starting at 3.59% for new vehicles and 7.22% for used vehicles, with an average loan amount of $18,000 per vehicle. Members can access online banking and mobile platforms to manage accounts remotely, pay bills, transfer funds to external accounts, and access e-statements. The credit union also promotes a First Time Homebuyer Program, though current mortgage lending is not actively offered. Additional services include brokered certificates of deposit through third-party partners and various financial calculators.

Transit FCU's distinguishing characteristics include its credit union structure, which typically offers member-friendly terms and lower fees compared to traditional banks. The institution serves a localized geographic area centered in Valley Stream, which may appeal to community-focused members. NCUA data indicates the credit union has 3,130 active share savings accounts and 104 money market accounts totaling $3.7 million, suggesting a modest member base. The credit union emphasizes online banking capabilities and mobile access for modern account management.

However, potential members should note several limitations. NCUA data shows zero active checking accounts, which contradicts the website's promotion of checking services and raises questions about current product availability or data accuracy. Mortgage lending is explicitly not offered despite prominent website mentions of a First Time Homebuyer Program and mortgage calculators. Auto lending appears limited, with the credit union serving only 4+ new auto loan members and 6+ used auto loan members, suggesting small lending volume. The institution's localized focus may limit accessibility for those outside the Valley Stream service area.
services: Checking accounts (Share Draft Accounts with debit card and check access)
Regular share savings accounts with competitive interest rates
Money market accounts with higher interest rates and flexible access
Online banking platform with bill pay and fund transfers
Mobile banking application for account management on the go
New auto loans with rates starting at 3.59%
Used auto loans with rates starting at 7.22%
External account transfers for multi-bank fund management
First Time Homebuyer Program with resources and guidance
Brokered certificates of deposit through third-party partners
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 3.59% for new vehicles and 7.22% for used vehicles
Credit union membership model typically offers lower fees and member-focused benefits
Comprehensive online and mobile banking platform for account management and bill pay
Money market accounts offering higher interest rates with flexible access
First Time Homebuyer Program with resources and favorable terms for new homeowners
Electronic statements (e-Statements) for paperless account management
Local branch presence in Valley Stream with direct phone support
Brokered certificates of deposit for investment diversification
cons: NCUA data shows zero active checking accounts despite website promotion of Share Draft Accounts, indicating possible product unavailability or data inconsistencies
Mortgages are explicitly not offered, contradicting website marketing of mortgage services and a First Time Homebuyer Program
Very small lending volume (4+ new auto loans, 6+ used auto loans) suggests limited lending capacity and experience
Geographically limited service area centered in Valley Stream, NY, may restrict accessibility for non-local members
Modest membership base (3,130 savings accounts) indicates limited institutional resources compared to larger financial institutions
best_for: Valley Stream, NY residents seeking local credit union membership with checking and savings accounts
Consumers in the service area looking to finance new or used vehicles with competitive rates
Members interested in money market accounts and brokered CDs for higher-yield savings
First-time homebuyers seeking educational resources and favorable lending terms
diagnosis: Transit Federal Credit Union is best for Valley Stream residents seeking a local credit union with basic checking, savings, and auto lending services. Primary caveats include data inconsistencies regarding checking account availability, no active mortgage lending despite website promotion, very small lending volume, and geographic restrictions that may limit accessibility for members outside the immediate service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
